mapping.a_element: statistical fluctuations in time-averaged properties of many-particle systems driven to nonequilibrium states
mapping.b_element: fluctuations of the unique non-equilibrium steady state in driven mechanical systems

mapping.a_element: fluctuation theorems providing analytical expressions for nonequilibrium states
mapping.b_element: existence, uniqueness, and properties (e.g., energy flux, convergence) of non-equilibrium steady states under general conditions

mapping.a_element: irreversibility emerging from reversible dynamics in finite systems
mapping.b_element: exponentially fast convergence to a unique non-equilibrium steady state in driven systems

brief_justification: While both abstracts address nonequilibrium systems and their fluctuations, Paper A focuses on fluctuation theorems as exact statistical mechanical relationships governing irreversibility and free energy changes, whereas Paper B emphasizes the existence, uniqueness, and convergence properties of non-equilibrium steady states using controllability and Hörmander conditions. The underlying machinery differs: fluctuation theorems rely on symmetry principles and time-reversal, while Paper B uses stochastic analysis and PDE techniques.
